name: Vel
description: The Vel, or spear, is a divine weapon and symbol of power, wisdom, and protection in Hindu mythology, primarily associated with the war god Kartikeya (also known as Murugan or Skanda). Forged by Lord Shiva himself, or in some versions, by Agni, the god of fire, the Vel was presented to Kartikeya to vanquish the demon Surapadman and his formidable army. The Vel is not merely a weapon of destruction; it represents the piercing intellect that cuts through ignorance and delusion. It embodies knowledge, discrimination, and the power to overcome obstacles. The Vel's sharp point symbolizes focus and clarity, guiding one towards righteousness and truth. Often depicted as a silver or golden spear, the Vel possesses immense cosmic energy and is venerated as a sacred object in temples dedicated to Kartikeya. The Vel serves as a constant reminder of the importance of courage, wisdom, and the eternal battle between good and evil. Its presence is believed to bring blessings of protection, prosperity, and spiritual growth to devotees. The power of the Vel is not just in its physical form, but in the divine energy and intent imbued within it, making it a potent symbol of victory over adversity and the triumph of dharma.
subtitle: Divine Spear of Kartikeya
origin: Forged by Shiva or Agni and given to Kartikeya
currentLocation: Worshipped in Kartikeya temples and homes

keyMyths.title: Defeat of Surapadman
keyMyths.description: Kartikeya received the Vel to defeat the demon Surapadman and his army. The Vel enabled Kartikeya to overcome the seemingly insurmountable forces of evil, restoring balance to the cosmos.

keyMyths.title: Vel as Symbol of Wisdom
keyMyths.description: The Vel is not only a weapon but also represents wisdom and knowledge. Its sharp point symbolizes the piercing intellect that cuts through ignorance and delusion, guiding one towards truth.

sources.author: Swami Sivananda
sources.description: Details the importance of Kartikeya and the Vel.
sources.text: Details the importance of Kartikeya and the Vel.

sources.author: T.A. Gopinatha Rao
sources.description: Describes the iconography of Kartikeya and the representation of the Vel.
